danger_signals.visual_form: opponent defense positioning on field grid
danger_signals.meaning: indicator of incoming tackle/sack threat during play execution

danger_signals.visual_form: concentrated defender sprites in passing lane
danger_signals.meaning: high interception or incompletion risk on quarterback pass attempts

danger_signals.visual_form: defensive ends flanking offensive line edges
danger_signals.meaning: rushing threat to ball carrier on running plays

safety_signals.visual_form: receiver sprite separation from nearest defender on field grid
safety_signals.meaning: viable passing target with low interception risk

safety_signals.visual_form: lineman sprite positioning between offense and defense
safety_signals.meaning: protection window for quarterback development or running lane creation

safety_signals.visual_form: ball position proximity to opponent endzone
safety_signals.meaning: scoring opportunity likelihood based on yardage to goal

faction_indicators.color_identity: team-specific uniform colors (28 official NFL teams from 1990-91 season)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: official NFL team logos in endzone (16-bit versions)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opposing team uniforms distinguished through color and logo differentiation on field sprites
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: team locker room/sideline area identification; playbook-specific play formation visual layouts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: roster turnover reflects prior 1990-91 NFL season rosters; no 1991 rookies represented due to licensing timing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player attribute values (speed, strength, accuracy) correspond to actual NFL statistics from preceding season

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: rain and snow visual effects on field (16-bit versions); sunny default state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: weather impacts ball physics trajectory and player movement responsiveness

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: yard-line markers and distance-to-goal display; field grid positioning of player sprites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: play selection restrictions based on down/distance scenarios; statistical tracking of field position advantage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: endzone entry visualization; score display state changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: touchdown completion triggers end-of-play sequence; field goal attempt visualization through sprite animation

environmental_storytelling.example: inclusion of real NFL teams and players (Bo Jackson specifically featured) communicates game legitimacy; exclusion of unlicensed players (Jim Kelly, Randall Cunningham, Bernie Kosar represented as generic QB names) telegraphs licensing constraints within world-state
team-specific offensive and defensive playbook formations visually represented during play selection; expanded playbooks in sequel versions signal increased strategic complexity and team identity
team sideline positioning and locker room identification establish home/away context; endzone logo display (in 16-bit versions) reinforces team territorial control narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: play selection from playbook menu; audible capability to change pre-called plays (SNES/Genesis versions)
sprite pathfinding toward predetermined route; separation from defensive coverage based on play design
positioning to intercept receiver routes or tackle ball carrier; linebacker clustering behavior indicates aggressive blitz package activation
directional movement control to navigate open field; dive play option (16-bit versions) provides evasion mechanic variant
field goal attempt animation; user-controlled touchback mechanics (16-bit versions)

notes: Tecmo Super Bowl communicates world-state primarily through roster authenticity (official NFL teams/players from 1990-91 season) and field positioning mechanics. Visual faction identity derives from official team uniform colors and logos (16-bit versions). Danger/safety signals manifest as defender proximity and coverage alignment on field grid. The game's manifestation layer emphasizes sports-simulation authenticity: player attributes reflect real-world NFL statistics, weather systems affect field state (16-bit versions), and play outcomes depend on formation positioning rather than narrative world-state. No political faction or geopolitical tension signaling present—game world-state is purely sports-competition-based. Developer text (Akihiko Shimoji credit) and unused animations (False Start penalty referee) indicate development-phase manifestation choices not fully realized in shipping version.
